name: OpenAI Text-to-Speech
description: Generate high-quality voiceover audio using OpenAI TTS (gpt-4o-mini-tts) with natural-sounding voices and style control

skill.name: openai_tts
skill.description: Generate voiceover audio using OpenAI Text-to-Speech
skill.system_prompt:
You have access to OpenAI Text-to-Speech for generating voiceover narration.

Available voices:
- coral (warm, engaging — great for narration)
- alloy (neutral, balanced)
- ash (clear, authoritative)
- ballad (soft, storytelling)
- echo (calm, measured)
- fable (expressive, animated)
- nova (friendly, upbeat)
- onyx (deep, resonant)
- sage (wise, steady)
- shimmer (bright, energetic)
- verse (smooth, professional)

Tips:
- Use the 'instructions' parameter to control speaking style: 'Speak in a warm, curious narrator voice' or 'Sound excited and energetic'
- For YouTube Shorts, use a voice that matches the content tone
- The audio is saved as an MP3 file and the file path is returned
- A 150-word script generates roughly 60 seconds of audio
- Model 'gpt-4o-mini-tts' is the default (best quality/cost ratio)

tools.name: generate_voiceover
tools.description: Generate voiceover audio from text using OpenAI TTS. Returns the file path of the saved MP3 audio file.

text.description: The text to convert to speech (the voiceover script)

voice.description: Voice to use: coral, alloy, ash, ballad, echo, fable, nova, onyx, sage, shimmer, verse. Default: 'coral'

instructions.description: Style instructions for the voice (e.g., 'Speak in a warm, engaging narrator voice with a sense of wonder'). Optional.

tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

API_KEY="$VAR_OPENAI_API_KEY"
if [ -z "$API_KEY" ]; then
  echo '{"error": "OpenAI API key not configured. Set the '\''openai_api_key'\'' variable."}'
  exit 0
fi

TEXT="$ARG_TEXT"
VOICE="${ARG_VOICE:-coral}"
INSTRUCTIONS="${ARG_INSTRUCTIONS:-}"
SPEED="${ARG_SPEED:-1.0}"

# Create output file with .mp3 extension using a unique name.
# Uses python3 uuid4 for collision-free naming (mktemp doesn't support suffixes portably).
TTS_DIR="${AT_WORK_DIR:-/tmp}"
OUTPUT_FILE="${TTS_DIR}/at-tts-$(python3 -c 'import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4().hex[:12])').mp3"

# Build JSON payload
if [ -n "$INSTRUCTIONS" ]; then
  PAYLOAD=$(python3 -c "
import json, sys
print(json.dumps({
    'model': 'gpt-4o-mini-tts',
    'voice': sys.argv[1],
    'input': sys.argv[2],
    'instructions': sys.argv[3],
    'speed': float(sys.argv[4]),
    'response_format': 'mp3'
}))
" "$VOICE" "$TEXT" "$INSTRUCTIONS" "$SPEED")
else
  PAYLOAD=$(python3 -c "
import json, sys
print(json.dumps({
    'model': 'gpt-4o-mini-tts',
    'voice': sys.argv[1],
    'input': sys.argv[2],
    'speed': float(sys.argv[3]),
    'response_format': 'mp3'
}))
" "$VOICE" "$TEXT" "$SPEED")
fi

# Make API call — response is binary audio
HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -w '%{http_code}' -o "$OUTPUT_FILE" \
  -X POST https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/speech \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "$PAYLOAD")

if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ]; then
  ERROR_BODY=$(cat "$OUTPUT_FILE")
  rm -f "$OUTPUT_FILE"
  echo "{\"error\": \"OpenAI TTS API error (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)\", \"details\": $(echo "$ERROR_BODY" | python3 -c 'import sys,json; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))' 2>/dev/null || echo '"unknown"')}"
  exit 0
fi

# Get file size and estimate duration
SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$OUTPUT_FILE" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$OUTPUT_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")

# Try to get duration using ffprobe if available
DURATION="unknown"
if command -v ffprobe &> /dev/null; then
  DURATION=$(ffprobe -v quiet -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 "$OUTPUT_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
fi

echo "{\"audio_file\": \"$OUTPUT_FILE\", \"file_size_bytes\": $SIZE, \"duration_seconds\": \"$DURATION\", \"voice\": \"$VOICE\", \"format\": \"mp3\"}"
